codeReview.summary: The fix commit resolves the prior blocking findings for project-setup, cli-parsing-help, clone-command, and worktree-dot-shorthand, and the fuzzy-scoring behavior remains aligned with the mission contract formula. However, shell-init-install is still not fixed: `lab init` still uses a SHELL-only heuristic and does not use parent-process fallback when SHELL is unset.

issues.description: The shell-detection fix was applied in `src/shell.rs`, but `cmd_init` still calls local `detect_shell_for_init()` (`src/commands/init.rs:37`) which only checks `SHELL` for fish and otherwise defaults to Bash. This bypasses the required parent-process fallback behavior from the prior failure (`shell-init-install.json`) when `SHELL` is unset.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shell detection logic is split between `src/shell.rs` and `src/commands/init.rs`, but shared docs describe shell detection as a shell module concern. This duplication made it easy to patch the wrong path and leave init behavior unchanged.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md says `src/shell.rs` owns shell detection, while `src/commands/init.rs:37-55` contains separate init-only detection logic not documented in AGENTS.md.

summary: Fail: 5/6 prior blocking areas are addressed, but the shell init fallback issue remains blocking because `lab init` still does not use parent-process shell detection when `SHELL` is unset.
